title: storage-v3 #9: mutate retry rechecks domain preconditions (fixes parallel-claim duplicate)
body:
# Goal

Fix the `Storage::mutate` / `mutate_once` retry loop so it
rechecks domain preconditions on every retry, not just on the
first attempt.

End state: a verb with a domain precondition (claim requires
status==Open; unclaim requires assignee==Some; set_block_reason
requires status!=Closed; etc.) that loses a CAS race and
retries against a freshly-read record will re-evaluate the
precondition against THAT record. If the precondition no
longer holds, the retry surfaces a typed error instead of
silently producing a duplicate write.

# Why now

Surfaced by ticket 3's subagent (diagnosis on `add0646`'s
session). Bug exists in V2 as well — V2 hid it as a timing
flake because jj's bookmark lock + working-copy snapshot
broadened the race window. V3's per-issue-ref CAS narrows the
window but exposes the bug deterministically.

Concrete repro: `parallel_ready_claim_limit_one_assigns_two_different_ids`
(`crates/jjf/tests/claim.rs:439`) — two threads `jjf ready
--claim --limit 1` against a fresh V3 repo with two unblocked
issues. Both threads read ready list, both pick the top id,
both pass the pre-`mutate` Open-status check, both call
`mutate`. First thread wins CAS, lands `assignee=alice
status=InProgress`. Second thread's `mutate_once` gets
`ConcurrentWrite`, retries by re-reading the record (now
shows InProgress + alice), but blindly re-runs the user
closure `|rec| { rec.assignee = Some(alice); rec.status =
InProgress; ... }` — same values, lands a duplicate trailer,
exits 0. Test fails: both claims report the same id.

# Approach

Per the subagent's diagnosis, two candidate API shapes:

**Option A. Precheck closure.** `mutate(id, precheck, apply)`:

```rust
fn mutate<P, F>(&self, id: &IssueId, precheck: P, apply: F) -> Result<...>
where
    P: Fn(&Record) -> Result<(), Error>,
    F: Fn(&mut Record) -> Result<Vec<Op>, Error>,
```

On every retry, re-read → run `precheck` → if Ok, run `apply`
and commit. If `precheck` returns Err, surface it (typed
"issue is no longer claimable" / similar).

**Option B. Tri-state closure return.** `mutate(id, op)`:

```rust
enum Mutate {
    Write(Vec<Op>),
    Skip,              // precondition still satisfied but no-op
    Conflict(Error),   // precondition violated; surface to caller
}

fn mutate<F: Fn(&Record) -> Mutate>(&self, id: &IssueId, op: F) -> ...
```

Closure inspects the freshly-read record and returns the
right variant. Cleaner — closure already has the record;
precondition logic lives in one place.

**Recommend B.** Single closure call site per retry, one
returnable enum, no signature creep. Existing callers can
move their precondition check into the closure body.

Migration is mechanical: every site that calls `mutate`
today (every domain verb with a precondition — `claim`,
`unclaim`, `set_status`, `set_block_reason`, `set_assignee`,
`set_slug`, `block`, `unblock`, others) inlines its
pre-`mutate` precondition check into the closure.

`mutate_once` similarly gets the new closure shape.

# Scope

In scope:
- `Storage::mutate` and `Storage::mutate_once` signature
  change (closure now returns `Mutate` enum).
- Move the pre-`mutate` precondition check inline into the
  closure body for every domain verb that has one.
- Same fix applies to BOTH V2 and V3 mutate paths — the bug
  is in the retry contract, not in the storage shape.
- The repro test
  `parallel_ready_claim_limit_one_assigns_two_different_ids`
  is the acceptance smoke test.

Out of scope:
- Concurrent-write retry policy itself (the once-with-re-read
  pattern stays; only the closure contract changes).
- Trailer format, op vocabulary, JSON schemas, CLI surface.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `parallel_ready_claim_limit_one_assigns_two_different_ids`
      passes 5/5 runs against V3.
- [ ] Same test passes 5/5 runs against V2 (the V2 path was
      flaky-not-broken; the fix should make it reliable).
- [ ] Every `Storage::mutate` call site that pre-checked a
      precondition has moved that check into its closure
      body (grep audit).
- [ ] New tests cover at least one other precondition that
      could race: claim-on-closed, unclaim-already-unclaimed,
      block-already-blocked, etc.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.

# Pointers

- Diagnosis: `add0646`'s subagent session (closing comment
  trail).
- Repro: `crates/jjf/tests/claim.rs:439`.
- Code to change: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:3006–3035`
  (`mutate_once`), `mutate` (nearby).
- Every domain verb in `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` that
  reads the record before calling `mutate` to gate it on a
  precondition — those are the migration sites.

# Out-of-scope reminder

Do NOT remove the CAS retry. The retry is correct; only the
closure contract is broken. Keep the once-with-jittered-sleep
retry policy from commit `434a2f87`.
